architecture.summary: RefWalk is a unified retrieval framework for regulatory compliance QA that traverses cross-document citations within an Operational Knowledge Graph, anchored by shared topic queries. It retrieves multi-view candidate evidence sets, fuses them via max-based aggregation, and enforces per-rule attribution to explicitly map claims back to regulatory sources. The system operates on a structured procedural knowledge graph derived from national R&D regulations, contrasting with flat RAG baselines that fail to handle tiered authority structures and citation closure.

biblio.title: Citation-Closure Retrieval and Per-Rule Attribution for Real-World Regulatory Compliance Question Answering

claims.statement: Existing RAG systems struggle with regulatory compliance QA due to flattened citation edges, fragmented retrieval expansions, and fragile post-hoc attribution
RefWalk establishes a strong baseline with substantial improvements in retrieval recall and citation accuracy on RegOps-Bench
Existing systems exhibit saturation on flat-structure rules in U.S. health compliance (HIPAA) evaluation

RegOps-Bench features an Operational Knowledge Graph derived from complex national R&D regulations, requiring structured procedural lookups and evidence-set closure rather than entity resolution or case-law reasoning
RefWalk traverses cross-document citations, fuses multi-view candidates via max-based aggregation, and enforces per-rule attribution to explicitly map claims to sources
